zh.Naseby, Northamptonshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾納希比
zh.All Saints Churchyard, Naseby, Daventry: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼哥吉拉 沙智 亞德
zh.lifeSummary: 當George Yorke於1563年6月15日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾納希比時，他的父親John Yorke32歲，母親Agnes Ulinskas29歲。他於1594年10月24日與Sarah Frances Astyll在英國諾薩普頓史爾納希比結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1647年在他的家鄉享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Yorke于1563年6月15日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾納希比时，他的父亲John Yorke32岁，母亲Agnes Ulinskas29岁。他于1594年10月24日与Sarah Frances Astyll在英国諾薩普頓史爾納希比结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1647年在他的家乡享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Yorke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: York
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When George Yorke was born on 15 June 1563, in Naseby, Northamptonshire, England, his father, John Yorke, was 32 and his mother, Agnes Ulinskas, was 29. He married Sarah Frances Astyll on 24 October 1594, in Naseby, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1647, in his hometown, at the age of 84, and was buried in All Saints Churchyard, Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: George Yorkeが1563年6月15日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアナセビーで生まれた時，父John Yorkeは32歳，母Agnes Ulinskasは29歳であった。1594年10月24日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアナセビーでSarah Frances Astyllと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1647年に故郷で84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイアル サインツ チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints Churchyard, Naseby, Daventry: 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: George Yorke(이)가 1563년 6월 15일 영국 노르섬프토실르 나세비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Yorke(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Ulinskas(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1594년 10월 24일 영국 노르섬프토실르 나세비에서 Sarah Frances Astyll(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1647년 그의 고향에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.All Saints Churchyard, Naseby, Daventry: алл сеинц чурчярд, миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Yorke родился 15 июня 1563 г. в местности неисеби, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Yorke было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Ulinskas, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Frances Astyll 24 октября 1594 г. в местности неисеби, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1647 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц чурчярд, миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
